description_long: This is a two-story wooden cabin with a rectangular footprint and a flat, plank-style roof. The exterior is clad in vertically arranged wooden planks of varying shades, giving it a rustic and weathered appearance. The cabin features two rows of symmetrically placed, square-paned windows on each visible side, with the front facade including a centered wooden door on the lower level. The windows are framed with simple wooden trim, and the overall structure is boxy and utilitarian, lacking decorative elements. The roof is flat and slightly overhangs the walls, constructed from the same wood as the siding. The cabin's design suggests a straightforward, functional approach typical of rustic or frontier architecture.
description: A rustic, two-story wooden cabin with a flat plank roof, symmetrical windows, and a centered front door. The exterior is clad in weathered vertical wood planks, giving it a simple, utilitarian look.
description_view_0: This view shows the front facade with a centered wooden door and two rows of three square-paned windows, all symmetrically arranged.
description_view_1: This side view displays two rows of three windows, matching the front but without a door, emphasizing the cabin's boxy shape.
description_view_2: The rear view mirrors the window arrangement of the other sides, with two rows of three windows and no door present.
description_view_3: This view highlights the front facade with the centered door and symmetrical window arrangement, making it the primary entrance side.
attribute_roof_type: flat roof constructed from wooden planks with slight overhangs
attribute_window_arrangement: Six square-paned windows per visible side, arranged in two symmetrical horizontal rows of three, all of similar size and evenly spaced
attribute_door_placement: Single wooden door centered on the front facade, directly below the middle window of the upper row
